notes: Patterns ported from the original Cashiro TelebirrParser.kt (parser-core/src/main/kotlin/com/ritesh/parser/core/bank/TelebirrParser.kt). Telebirr is Ethio telecom's ETB mobile-money wallet (sender "127"). Notable behaviors preserved from the Kotlin parser: - Savings flows are reversed relative to the saving account: a deposit TO the saving account leaves the wallet (EXPENSE); a withdraw FROM the saving account enters the wallet (INCOME). Modeled as pipeline setFieldWhen steps so they win over the received/paid/transferred typeRules, exactly like the Kotlin `when` ordering. - The Kotlin parser used the name after "Dear" as the account identifier (e.g. "[Name]"); the engine's accountLast4 is digits-only, so like other wallets the manifest routes to the "0000" wallet pseudo-account instead.
